document_title: PR24 final determinations: Aligning risk and return — Allowed return Appendix

key_decisions.decision: CAPM framework retained for cost of equity
key_decisions.detail: Continued use of CAPM as primary methodology with cross-checks from MAR-implied cost of equity, broker WACC estimates, and financeability analysis
Based on index-linked gilts (ILGs), using 1-month and 6-month trailing averages of 20-year ILGs, yielding 1.52% real CPIH-stripped
6.68%–6.98% real CPIH-stripped, drawing on long-run historical ex-post and ex-ante evidence
Used Severn Trent plc and United Utilities plc as listed comparators; 2-year and 5-year estimation windows with daily and monthly frequencies

key_decisions.decision: Cost of equity point estimate above range
key_decisions.detail: Selected 5.10% point estimate, above the top of the 4.56%–5.07% CAPM range, reflecting aiming-up and cross-check evidence
Balance sheet-based benchmark using actual company debt portfolios and iBoxx indices; 2.77% allowed
Forward-looking benchmark based on iBoxx A/BBB 10yr+ non-financials with a forward adjustment; 3.74% allowed
Based on notional company refinancing profile over 2025-30

key_numbers.label: TMR low
key_numbers.context: Bottom of total market return range

key_numbers.label: TMR high
key_numbers.context: Top of total market return range

glossary_terms.definition: Index-Linked Gilt — UK government bond with returns linked to RPI inflation
Markit iBoxx indices — bond market benchmarks used to estimate cost of debt
Market-to-Asset Ratio — ratio of enterprise value to RCV, used as cross-check on allowed returns
Regulatory practice of setting the allowed return above the midpoint estimate to reduce the risk of under-investment
Converting nominal or RPI-linked returns to a real CPIH basis using the RPI-CPIH wedge
